skill.name: gemini-api-dev
skill.description: Comprehensive guide for building applications with Google Gemini API including multimodal content, function calling, and structured outputs
skill.summary: Complete reference for Gemini API development with SDK examples in Python, JavaScript, and Go
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analyzer flagged 45 patterns that are all false positives. This is a documentation-only skill (SKILL.md) containing code examples in markdown format. Backticks are markdown code fences, not shell execution. URLs are documentation references to official Google sources. No executable code or security risks present.

evidence.reasoning: Go import statements in documentation code example, not dynamic JavaScript import
Markdown code fence backticks for syntax-highlighted code examples, not shell command execution
Documentation URLs referencing official Google API endpoints and docs, not network calls made by skill

content.user_title: Build apps with Google Gemini API
content.value_statement: Developers need clear guidance on integrating Google's advanced AI models into their applications. This skill provides comprehensive documentation with SDK examples for Python, JavaScript, and Go to accelerate Gemini API development.
actual_capabilities: Access latest Gemini 3.0 models including Pro and Flash variants
Implement multimodal content processing for text, images, audio, and video
Configure function calling to enable model-driven API invocations
Generate structured JSON outputs matching custom schemas
Utilize context caching for improved performance with large contexts
Generate text embeddings for semantic search applications
limitations: This skill provides documentation guidance only, not executable API code
Requires separate Google API key and billing setup through Google AI Studio
Does not cover deprecated models like gemini-2.x, gemini-1.5.x series
Code examples require manual implementation in your development environment

use_cases.title: AI-powered application development
use_cases.description: Build chatbots, content generators, and intelligent assistants using Gemini's advanced language capabilities with production-ready SDK patterns
use_cases.target_user: Full-stack developers building AI-enhanced web applications

use_cases.title: Multimodal content analysis
use_cases.description: Process and analyze images, documents, audio, and video content alongside text prompts for comprehensive understanding
use_cases.target_user: Data scientists and ML engineers working with diverse content types

use_cases.title: Automated workflow integration
use_cases.description: Implement function calling to enable Gemini models to trigger external APIs, database queries, and business logic automatically
use_cases.target_user: Backend engineers creating intelligent automation systems

prompt_templates.title: Basic text generation
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the Gemini API to generate a response. Initialize the client with your API key, call generate_content with model 'gemini-3-flash-preview', and pass your text prompt. Handle the response text output.
prompt_templates.scenario: Quick start for simple chat or completion tasks

prompt_templates.title: Image analysis with Gemini
prompt_templates.prompt: Set up multimodal input by combining text and image data. Use the appropriate content parts for image data (base64 or URI), add your text question about the image, and send to a multimodal-capable model like gemini-3-pro-preview.
prompt_templates.scenario: Analyzing uploaded images, diagrams, or screenshots

prompt_templates.title: Function calling implementation
prompt_templates.prompt: Define your function schema with name, description, and parameters. Pass function_declarations to the API config. When the model responds with function_call, execute your function and return results. Continue the conversation with the function response.
prompt_templates.scenario: Building AI agents that can query databases or call external APIs

prompt_templates.title: Structured JSON output
prompt_templates.prompt: Configure response_mime_type to 'application/json' and provide a response_schema defining your expected JSON structure. The model will generate valid JSON matching your schema with proper types and required fields.
prompt_templates.scenario: Extracting structured data from unstructured text or generating API-ready payloads

output_examples.input: Generate a product description for a wireless headphone with noise cancellation
output_examples.output: Experience immersive sound with our premium wireless headphones featuring active noise cancellation. Advanced 40mm drivers deliver deep bass and crystal-clear highs while adaptive ANC technology blocks ambient noise for focused listening.

output_examples.input: Analyze this chart image and summarize the trend
output_examples.output: The line chart shows quarterly revenue growth from Q1 to Q4. Revenue increased steadily from 2.3M to 4.1M, representing 78% year-over-year growth. The steepest increase occurred between Q2 and Q3.

output_examples.input: Extract entities from this news article as JSON
output: Structured JSON response with fields: article_title, publication_date, key_people (array), organizations (array), locations (array), and summary

best_practices: Always use v1beta API version as the default unless specifically required to use v1
Refer to the official REST API discovery spec as the source of truth for field names and types
Fetch the llms.txt documentation index to discover available API features and endpoints
Migrate from deprecated SDKs (google-generativeai, @google/generative-ai) to new packages
anti_patterns: Using legacy model names like gemini-2.0 or gemini-1.5 which are deprecated
Hardcoding API keys directly in source code instead of using environment variables
Assuming all Gemini models support the same features without checking model capabilities
Ignoring rate limits and quota constraints when making production API calls

faq.question: Which Gemini models should I use for new projects?
faq.answer: Use gemini-3-pro-preview for complex reasoning and coding tasks, or gemini-3-flash-preview for fast, cost-effective responses. Avoid deprecated 2.x and 1.5.x models.

faq.question: How do I get an API key for Gemini?
faq.answer: Visit Google AI Studio, create a project, and generate an API key. Free tier includes generous quotas for development and testing.

faq.question: What is the difference between v1 and v1beta API versions?
faq.answer: v1beta is the default and recommended version with latest features. Use v1 only when your integration specifically requires stable API behavior without beta features.

faq.question: Can Gemini process images and documents?
faq.answer: Yes, Gemini supports multimodal input including images, audio, video, PDFs, and text documents. Use the appropriate content parts to include non-text data in your requests.

faq.question: How does function calling work with Gemini?
faq.answer: Define your function schema in the API request. When Gemini determines a function should be called, it returns a function_call response. Execute your function and send results back to continue the conversation.

faq.question: What SDK should I use for my programming language?
faq.answer: Use google-genai for Python, @google/genai for JavaScript/TypeScript, or google.golang.org/genai for Go. Legacy packages google-generativeai and @google/generative-ai are deprecated.
